title: Chastain Park Spring Arts Festival
description: Annual outdoor arts & craft festival with 200+ vendors — paintings, ceramics, jewelry, photography, live demos. It's Mother's Day weekend so it's extra lively. Free to walk the grounds, food trucks on site. Arrive anytime between 10 AM–5 PM, plan 3–4 hours to really browse and soak it in. Great people-watching and easy conversation-starter energy.
budget: Free to attend, $10–$40 for art/food purchases
location: Chastain Park, 4465 Stella Dr NW, Atlanta — Sat May 9, 10 AM–6 PM
vibe: Outdoorsy, artistic, relaxed — excellent for couples who enjoy walking and browsing together

title: Best of Atlanta Comedy Showcase — Laughing Skull Lounge
description: Top local Atlanta comedians in a tight 75-minute showcase. Intimate venue, 21+ crowd, full bar. Perfect for a fun night out — laughter is basically a bonding exercise. Shows at 8 PM (doors 7:30), easy to pair with a late dinner nearby in Virginia-Highland after.
location: Laughing Skull Lounge, 878 Virginia Ave NE, Atlanta — Sat May 9, 8 PM
vibe: Fun, intimate, high-energy laugh — great second-half date move

title: Dinner at La Semilla + Evening Stroll in O4W
description: Atlanta's most beloved vegetarian/Latin fusion spot — try the arepas, plantain bowls, and whatever's seasonal. Reservations recommended for Saturday dinner. After, walk the BeltLine fromoire Memorial Dr through the Eastside Trail — murals, street art, string lights, and good people energy. Ends near Krog Street Market if you want a nightcap or dessert. Full afternoon-to-evening date that doesn't feel like you're doing one thing.
budget: $40–$65 per person for dinner, BeltLine is free to walk
location: La Semilla, 780 Memorial Dr SE, Atlanta — Sat May 9, dinner from 6 PM; BeltLine after
vibe: Foodie, walkable, creative neighborhood — great for couples who love food and exploring
